```{r}
    subtitle = "Útil cuando quieres reforzar volumen acumulado",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Valor"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-orden-stacked
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Categorías ordenadas: stacked area"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(budget_long, aes(year, value, fill = area)) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.88, color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = budget_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Stacked area",
    subtitle = "Muestra composición cambiante a lo largo del tiempo",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Valor",
    fill = NULL
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-orden-stream
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Categorías ordenadas: stream graph"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(stream_budget, aes(year, group = area, fill = area)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), alpha = 0.9, color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = budget_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Stream graph",
    subtitle = "Variante expresiva del stacked area con base centrada",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

## Jerarquías, anidación y estructura

Cuando las categorías se organizan en niveles, lo importante ya no es solo el tamaño sino también la pertenencia y la similitud entre grupos.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-jerarquia
#| include: false

nest_counts <- mpg |>
  count(manufacturer, class, name = "value") |>
  filter(manufacturer %in% c("dodge", "ford", "toyota", "volkswagen"))

manu_order <- nest_counts |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total))

manu_cols <- setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(manu_order)], manu_order$manufacturer)

inner_sun <- manu_order |>
  mutate(
    ymax = cumsum(total),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0),
    xmin = 1,
    xmax = 2
  )

outer_sun <- nest_counts |>
  left_join(inner_sun |> select(manufacturer, ymin_inner = ymin), by = "manufacturer") |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  arrange(desc(value), .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymax = ymin_inner + cumsum(value),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = first(ymin_inner)),
    xmin = 2,
    xmax = 3
  ) |>
  ungroup()

p_sunburst_cat <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = inner_sun,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = outer_sun,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.35,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Expone jerarquías en anillos concéntricos"
  )

profile_manufacturer <- mpg |>
  count(manufacturer, class) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)

hc_manufacturer <- hclust(
  dist(scale(select(profile_manufacturer, -manufacturer))),
  method = "ward.D2"
)

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-jerarquia-sunburst
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Jerarquías: sunburst"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = inner_sun,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = outer_sun,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.35,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Expone jerarquías en anillos concéntricos"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-jerarquia-dendro
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Jerarquías: dendrograma"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

plot(
  hc_manufacturer,
  labels = profile_manufacturer$manufacturer,
  main = "Dendrograma",
  xlab = "",
  sub = ""
)
```

## Adyacencia, enlaces y flujos entre categorías

Esta familia aparece cuando el dato principal ya no es un valor aislado, sino una relación entre nodos o bloques.

En esta sección se mantiene la familia visual completa porque sigue siendo útil ver cómo cambia la lectura cuando el enlace surge entre categorías con peso numérico. La versión de referencia para esta gramática visual reaparece más adelante en el atlas relacional, donde la estructura de red pasa a ser el foco principal.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-adyacencia
#| include: false

link_df <- tribble(
  ~from,         ~to,            ~value,
  "Ventas",      "Marketing",    8,
  "Ventas",      "Soporte",      5,
  "Marketing",   "Producto",     6,
  "Producto",    "Operaciones",  7,
  "Soporte",     "Producto",     3,
  "Operaciones", "Finanzas",     5,
  "Soporte",     "Finanzas",     2
)

nodes <- tibble(name = unique(c(link_df$from, link_df$to)))

adj_long <- bind_rows(
  link_df,
  transmute(link_df, from = to, to = from, value = value)
) |>
  complete(from = nodes$name, to = nodes$name, fill = list(value = 0))

p_adj_heat <- ggplot(adj_long, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))),
    size = 3,
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#EFE3C8", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap de adyacencia",
    subtitle = "Hace legible una matriz de conexiones",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))

nodes_line <- nodes |>
  mutate(x = seq_len(n()), y = 0)

arc_df <- link_df |>
  left_join(nodes_line, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(nodes_line, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_arc_cat <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = arc_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(data = nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.4, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(nodes)], nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.1), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Útil cuando los nodos tienen un orden lineal"
  )

nodes_circle <- nodes |>
  mutate(
    angle = seq(pi / 2, pi / 2 + 2 * pi - 2 * pi / n(), length.out = n()),
    x = cos(angle),
    y = sin(angle)
  )

chord_df <- link_df |>
  left_join(nodes_circle, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(nodes_circle, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_chord_cat <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = chord_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.23,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(data = nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.5, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(
    data = nodes_circle,
    aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name),
    size = 2.9,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(nodes)], nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Versión circular para enfatizar conectividad global"
  )

net_pos <- tibble(
  name = c("Ventas", "Marketing", "Producto", "Operaciones", "Soporte", "Finanzas"),
  x = c(0.0, 1.0, 1.8, 2.5, 0.6, 3.1),
  y = c(0.7, 1.7, 0.8, -0.1, -0.7, 0.2)
)

network_df <- link_df |>
  left_join(net_pos, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(net_pos, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_network_cat <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = network_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value),
    color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.7)
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = net_pos,
    aes(x, y),
    size = 7,
    color = PAL_BG,
    fill = PAL_MUST,
    shape = 21,
    stroke = 1
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = net_pos,
    aes(x, y, label = name),
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La opción más directa cuando la relación es el dato principal"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-adyacencia-heat
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Adyacencia: heatmap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(adj_long, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))),
    size = 3,
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#EFE3C8", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap de adyacencia",
    subtitle = "Hace legible una matriz de conexiones",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-adyacencia-arc
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Adyacencia: arc"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 3.9

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = arc_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(data = nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.4, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(nodes)], nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.1), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Útil cuando los nodos tienen un orden lineal"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-adyacencia-chord
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Adyacencia: chord"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = chord_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.23,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(data = nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.5, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(
    data = nodes_circle,
    aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name),
    size = 2.9,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(nodes)], nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Versión circular para enfatizar conectividad global"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-catnum-adyacencia-network
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico + numérico · Adyacencia: network"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = network_df,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value),
    color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.7)
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = net_pos,
    aes(x, y),
    size = 7,
    color = PAL_BG,
    fill = PAL_MUST,
    shape = 21,
    stroke = 1
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = net_pos,
    aes(x, y, label = name),
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La opción más directa cuando la relación es el dato principal"
  )
```

## Cómo leer este segundo atlas

La clave aquí es no duplicar decisiones: si el gráfico ya aparece en el atlas numérico o en el categórico, úsalo con el mismo criterio. Lo que cambia es la mezcla de variable discreta + valor.

`Primera opción` en categórico + numérico:

-   `barplot` y `lollipop` cuando cada categoría ya está resumida en un valor.
-   `boxplot` cuando cada grupo contiene varias observaciones.
-   `grouped scatter` o `heatmap` cuando comparas varias métricas o subgrupos.
-   `line plot` cuando hay orden temporal o secuencial.
-   `treemap` o `stacked barplot` cuando la composición es parte central del mensaje.

`Especializados` en categórico + numérico:

-   `waffle`, `pie`, `doughnut`, `word cloud` y `circular packing` como recursos más expresivos que analíticos.
-   `violin`, `ridgeline`, `density` e `histogram` cuando necesitas forma completa de la distribución.
-   `parallel plot`, `spider plot`, `PCA` y `correlogram` cuando el perfil multivariado es más importante que una sola medida.
-   `connected scatter`, `area`, `stacked area` y `stream graph` cuando además del valor importa trayectoria o acumulación.
-   `sunburst`, `dendrogram`, `arc`, `chord`, `network` y `sankey` cuando el dato ya empieza a comportarse como jerarquía o red.

::: callout-tip
## Regla de decisión

En datos categóricos con valor numérico, decide primero si necesitas comparar, componer, perfilar, ordenar, anidar o conectar. Esa decisión reduce mucho más el espacio de opciones que empezar buscando un `geom`.
:::

# 13 · Atlas de gráficos categóricos

La imagen nueva separa un caso que suele mezclarse con demasiada facilidad: datos puramente categóricos. Aquí la pregunta ya no es “¿cuántas continuas tengo?”, sino “¿quiero comparar categorías, mostrar solapamiento, revelar jerarquías, perfilar subgrupos o enseñar conexiones?”.

También conviene notar que varios gráficos se repiten respecto a la sección anterior. Eso no es un error: un mismo recurso visual puede servir tanto para perfiles categórico-numéricos como para estructuras enteramente categóricas. Aquí la diferencia es que el foco ya no está en el valor numérico, sino en la organización de categorías, pertenencias y relaciones discretas.

## Una sola variable categórica

Cuando solo quieres comparar frecuencias o proporciones, el repertorio base incluye barras, lollipops, waffles y varias alternativas de composición.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-a
#| include: false

cut_counts <- ggplot2::diamonds |>
  count(cut, sort = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    cut = as.character(cut),
    prop = n / sum(n)
  )

cut_cols <- setNames(cat_palette[seq_len(nrow(cut_counts))], cut_counts$cut)

waffle_cut <- cut_counts |>
  mutate(tiles = round(prop * 100)) |>
  uncount(tiles) |>
  mutate(
    tile = row_number() - 1,
    col = tile %% 10,
    row = tile %/% 10
  )

p_waffle_categ <- ggplot(waffle_cut, aes(col, -row, fill = cut)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.35) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Waffle",
    subtitle = "Convierte proporciones en una cuadrícula comparable",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_bar_categ <- ggplot(cut_counts |> arrange(n), aes(n, fct_reorder(cut, n), fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Barplot",
    subtitle = "La mejor referencia cuando importa comparar magnitudes",
    x = "Frecuencia",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

p_lollipop_categ <- ggplot(cut_counts |> arrange(n), aes(n, fct_reorder(cut, n), color = cut)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = n, y = cut, yend = cut),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    color = PAL_GRID
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Lollipop",
    subtitle = "Reduce tinta y sigue siendo preciso para ranking",
    x = "Frecuencia",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

word_cut <- cut_counts |>
  mutate(
    x = c(-1.0, 0.85, -0.25, 1.1, -0.85),
    y = c(0.9, 0.82, 0.1, -0.6, -0.85),
    angle = c(0, 0, -8, 10, 0)
  )

p_word_categ <- ggplot(word_cut, aes(x, y, label = cut, size = n, color = cut, angle = angle)) +
  geom_text(fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  scale_size(range = c(10, 19)) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Word cloud",
    subtitle = "Útil como recurso expresivo, no como herramienta precisa"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-b
#| include: false

pack_cut <- cut_counts |>
  mutate(
    x = c(0.25, 0.58, 0.8, 0.39, 0.67),
    y = c(0.7, 0.76, 0.55, 0.3, 0.2),
    r = rescale(sqrt(n), to = c(0.13, 0.27))
  )

pack_cut_poly <- pmap_dfr(
  list(pack_cut$cut, pack_cut$x, pack_cut$y, pack_cut$r),
  function(cut, x0, y0, r) {
    circle_polygon(x0, y0, r) |>
      mutate(cut = cut)
  }
)

p_cpack_categ <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_cut_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = cut, fill = cut),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = pack_cut,
    aes(x, y, label = cut),
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing",
    subtitle = "Sugiere parte-todo, pero dificulta la comparación fina"
  )

p_doughnut_categ <- ggplot(cut_counts, aes(x = 2, y = n, fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.9, color = PAL_BG) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0.5, 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Doughnut",
    subtitle = "Mejor solo con pocas categorías y mensajes simples"
  )

p_pie_categ <- ggplot(cut_counts, aes(x = 1, y = n, fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = PAL_BG) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Pie",
    subtitle = "Solo funciona bien cuando las partes son muy distintas"
  )

tree_cut <- simple_treemap_layout(
  cut_counts |>
    transmute(cut = as.character(cut), value = n),
  value_col = "value"
)

p_tree_categ <- ggplot(tree_cut) +
  geom_rect(
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = cut),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = cut),
    size = 3.1,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Compacta muchas partes y aprovecha mejor el espacio"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-waffle
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: waffle"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(waffle_cut, aes(col, -row, fill = cut)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.35) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Waffle",
    subtitle = "Convierte proporciones en una retícula de 100 unidades",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-bar
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: barplot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(cut_counts |> arrange(n), aes(n, fct_reorder(cut, n), fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Barplot",
    subtitle = "La referencia principal para comparar categorías",
    x = "Frecuencia",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-lollipop
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: lollipop"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(cut_counts |> arrange(n), aes(n, fct_reorder(cut, n), color = cut)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = n, y = cut, yend = cut),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    color = PAL_GRID
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  labs(
    title = "Lollipop",
    subtitle = "Reduce tinta y sigue siendo preciso para ranking",
    x = "Frecuencia",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-word
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: word cloud"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(word_cut, aes(x, y, label = cut, size = n, color = cut, angle = angle)) +
  geom_text(fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cut_cols) +
  scale_size(range = c(10, 19)) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Word cloud",
    subtitle = "Útil como recurso expresivo, no como herramienta precisa"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-packing
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: circular packing"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_cut_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = cut, fill = cut),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = pack_cut,
    aes(x, y, label = cut),
    size = 3,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing",
    subtitle = "Sugiere parte-todo, pero dificulta la comparación fina"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-doughnut
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: doughnut"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(cut_counts, aes(x = 2, y = n, fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.9, color = PAL_BG) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0.5, 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Doughnut",
    subtitle = "Mejor solo con pocas categorías y mensajes simples"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-pie
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: pie"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(cut_counts, aes(x = 1, y = n, fill = cut)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = PAL_BG) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Pie",
    subtitle = "Solo funciona bien cuando las partes son muy distintas"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-una-treemap
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Una variable: treemap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(tree_cut) +
  geom_rect(
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = cut),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = cut),
    size = 3.1,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cut_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Compacta muchas partes y aprovecha mejor el espacio"
  )
```

## Dos listas independientes

Cuando las categorías forman conjuntos con solapamiento, el caso más clásico es el diagrama de Venn.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-venn
#| fig-cap: "Dos listas independientes: diagrama de Venn"
#| fig-width: 7.4
#| fig-height: 5.2

venn_a <- circle_polygon(-0.38, 0, 0.72) |>
  mutate(set = "Exploración")

venn_b <- circle_polygon(0.38, 0, 0.72) |>
  mutate(set = "Comunicación")

venn_df <- bind_rows(venn_a, venn_b)

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = venn_df,
    aes(x, y, group = set, fill = set),
    alpha = 0.45,
    color = PAL_MID,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = -0.86, y = 0.88, label = "Exploración", fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.86, y = 0.88, label = "Comunicación", fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = -0.52, y = 0, label = "18", color = PAL_DARK, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.52, y = 0, label = "14", color = PAL_DARK, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "7", color = PAL_DARK, size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Exploración" = PAL_CAN, "Comunicación" = "#C96C5B")) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Venn diagram",
    subtitle = "Resume pertenencia compartida entre conjuntos categóricos"
  )
```

## Categorías anidadas

Si unas categorías contienen a otras, los gráficos jerárquicos pasan a ser más informativos que una simple tabla cruzada.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-a
#| include: false

nested_counts <- mpg |>
  count(manufacturer, class, name = "value") |>
  filter(manufacturer %in% c("dodge", "ford", "toyota", "volkswagen")) |>
  mutate(
    manufacturer = str_to_title(manufacturer),
    class = str_to_title(class)
  )

manu_totals <- nested_counts |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total))

manu_cols <- setNames(cat_palette[seq_len(nrow(manu_totals))], manu_totals$manufacturer)

p_nested_bar <- ggplot(nested_counts, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  labs(
    title = "Barplot anidado",
    subtitle = "Primero compara el total y luego la composición interna",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Clase"
  )

pack_nested <- nested_counts |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  mutate(
    x = case_match(
      manufacturer,
      "Dodge" ~ 0.22,
      "Ford" ~ 0.72,
      "Toyota" ~ 0.28,
      "Volkswagen" ~ 0.73
    ),
    y = case_match(
      manufacturer,
      "Dodge" ~ 0.73,
      "Ford" ~ 0.74,
      "Toyota" ~ 0.26,
      "Volkswagen" ~ 0.24
    ),
    base_r = case_match(
      manufacturer,
      "Dodge" ~ 0.18,
      "Ford" ~ 0.18,
      "Toyota" ~ 0.16,
      "Volkswagen" ~ 0.15
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(value), .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    dx = c(-0.07, 0.06, -0.01, 0.08, -0.08, 0.02, 0.06, -0.04)[seq_len(n())],
    dy = c(0.05, 0.03, -0.06, -0.03, 0.09, -0.02, 0.06, -0.07)[seq_len(n())],
    r = rescale(sqrt(value), to = c(0.035, 0.11))
  ) |>
  ungroup()

pack_nested_poly <- pmap_dfr(
  list(pack_nested$class, pack_nested$x + pack_nested$dx, pack_nested$y + pack_nested$dy, pack_nested$r, pack_nested$manufacturer),
  function(class, x0, y0, r, manufacturer) {
    circle_polygon(x0, y0, r) |>
      mutate(class = class, manufacturer = manufacturer)
  }
)

p_nested_pack <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_nested_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(manufacturer, class), fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = distinct(pack_nested, manufacturer, x, y),
    aes(x, y, label = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_DARK,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing anidado",
    subtitle = "Expone inclusión jerárquica, no comparación exacta"
  )

inner_nested <- manu_totals |>
  mutate(
    ymax = cumsum(total),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0),
    xmin = 1,
    xmax = 2
  )

outer_nested <- nested_counts |>
  left_join(inner_nested |> select(manufacturer, ymin_inner = ymin), by = "manufacturer") |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  arrange(desc(value), .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymax = ymin_inner + cumsum(value),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = first(ymin_inner)),
    xmin = 2,
    xmax = 3
  ) |>
  ungroup()

p_nested_sun <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = inner_nested,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = outer_nested,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.3,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Jerarquía expresada en anillos concéntricos"
  )

tree_nested <- simple_treemap_layout(
  manu_totals |>
    transmute(manufacturer, value = total),
  value_col = "value"
)

p_nested_tree <- ggplot(tree_nested) +
  geom_rect(
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = manufacturer),
    size = 3.2,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Compacta jerarquías con muy buen uso del área"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-b
#| fig-cap: "Categorías anidadas: dendrograma"
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5.4

profile_nested <- nested_counts |>
  select(manufacturer, class, value) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = class, values_from = value, values_fill = 0)

hc_nested <- hclust(
  dist(scale(select(profile_nested, -manufacturer))),
  method = "ward.D2"
)

plot(
  hc_nested,
  labels = profile_nested$manufacturer,
  main = "Dendrograma de similitud entre fabricantes",
  xlab = "",
  sub = ""
)
```

## Subgrupos y tablas cruzadas

Cuando las categorías se cruzan entre sí, puedes leer patrones con puntos, tiles, barras y diagramas de perfil.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-a
#| include: false

cross_counts <- mpg |>
  count(class, drv, name = "value") |>
  filter(class %in% c("compact", "midsize", "pickup", "suv")) |>
  mutate(
    class = str_to_title(class),
    drv = recode(drv, `4` = "4x4", f = "Delantera", r = "Trasera")
  )

drv_cols2 <- setNames(cat_palette[1:3], c("4x4", "Delantera", "Trasera"))

p_grouped_scatter_cat <- ggplot(cross_counts, aes(value, class, color = drv)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Grouped scatter",
    subtitle = "Comparación ligera entre subgrupos",
    x = "Conteo",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Tracción"
  )

p_heat_cross <- ggplot(cross_counts, aes(drv, class, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), color = PAL_DARK, fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F2E7D3", high = PAL_MUST) +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap",
    subtitle = "Hace visible una tabla cruzada en segundos",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Conteo"
  )

p_lollipop_cross <- ggplot(cross_counts, aes(value, class, color = drv)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = value, y = class, yend = class),
    linewidth = 1.1,
    color = PAL_GRID,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3.8, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Lollipop por subgrupo",
    subtitle = "Útil cuando quieres comparar pocos subgrupos por categoría",
    x = "Conteo",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Tracción"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-b
#| include: false

profile_categ <- mpg |>
  filter(manufacturer %in% c("dodge", "ford", "toyota", "volkswagen")) |>
  count(manufacturer, drv, name = "value") |>
  mutate(
    manufacturer = str_to_title(manufacturer),
    drv = recode(drv, `4` = "4x4", f = "Delantera", r = "Trasera")
  )

manu_cols2 <- setNames(cat_palette[1:4], c("Dodge", "Ford", "Toyota", "Volkswagen"))

profile_scaled <- profile_categ |>
  group_by(drv) |>
  mutate(value_scaled = rescale(value, to = c(0.15, 1))) |>
  ungroup()

p_parallel_cat <- ggplot(profile_scaled, aes(drv, value_scaled, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel plot",
    subtitle = "Compara el perfil relativo de cada categoría principal",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Escala relativa",
    color = NULL
  )

spider_cat <- profile_scaled |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  arrange(drv) |>
  group_modify(~ bind_rows(.x, slice(.x, 1))) |>
  ungroup()

p_spider_cat <- ggplot(spider_cat, aes(drv, value_scaled, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer, fill = manufacturer)) +
  geom_polygon(alpha = 0.12, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2.2) +
  coord_polar() +
  scale_color_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Spider plot",
    subtitle = "Muy llamativo, menos preciso que el paralelo",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())

p_grouped_bar_cat2 <- ggplot(profile_categ, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = drv)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), width = 0.66) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Grouped barplot",
    subtitle = "La opción robusta para comparación entre subgrupos",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Tracción"
  )

p_stacked_bar_cat2 <- ggplot(profile_categ, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = drv)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.68) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Stacked barplot",
    subtitle = "Sirve mejor para composición que para comparación fina",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Tracción"
  )

left_prof <- profile_categ |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_prof <- profile_categ |>
  left_join(left_prof, by = "manufacturer") |>
  group_by(manufacturer) |>
  arrange(drv, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymin0 = ymin + cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
    ymax0 = ymin0 + value
  ) |>
  ungroup()

right_prof <- profile_categ |>
  group_by(drv) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_prof2 <- flow_prof |>
  left_join(right_prof |> rename(ymin_r = ymin, ymax_r = ymax), by = "drv") |>
  group_by(drv) |>
  arrange(manufacturer, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymin1 = ymin_r + cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
    ymax1 = ymin1 + value
  ) |>
  ungroup()

sankey_prof <- pmap_dfr(
  list(flow_prof2$manufacturer, flow_prof2$drv, flow_prof2$ymin0, flow_prof2$ymax0, flow_prof2$ymin1, flow_prof2$ymax1),
  function(manufacturer, drv, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) {
    make_flow_ribbon(0.1, 0.9, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) |>
      mutate(manufacturer = manufacturer, drv = drv)
  }
)

p_sankey_categ <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_prof,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(manufacturer, drv), fill = manufacturer),
    alpha = 0.78,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_prof, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_prof, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_prof, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = manufacturer), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_prof, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = drv), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols2, guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey diagram",
    subtitle = "Muestra cómo se reparte cada categoría principal entre subgrupos"
  )

```

## Adyacencia entre categorías

Cuando el dato clave es la relación entre categorías, la familia cambia: aparecen `heatmap`, `arc`, `chord`, `network` y de nuevo `sankey`.

La lógica visual es la misma que en el atlas categórico + numérico y vuelve a consolidarse después en el atlas relacional. Aquí conviene leer estos ejemplos como variantes categóricas de una familia común: cambia el tipo de dato, no la gramática base del gráfico.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-adyacencia
#| include: false

cat_links <- tribble(
  ~from,        ~to,          ~value,
  "Ventas",     "Marketing",  8,
  "Ventas",     "Soporte",    5,
  "Marketing",  "Producto",   6,
  "Producto",   "Operaciones",7,
  "Soporte",    "Producto",   3,
  "Operaciones","Finanzas",   5,
  "Soporte",    "Finanzas",   2
)

cat_nodes <- tibble(name = unique(c(cat_links$from, cat_links$to)))

cat_adj <- bind_rows(
  cat_links,
  transmute(cat_links, from = to, to = from, value = value)
) |>
  complete(from = cat_nodes$name, to = cat_nodes$name, fill = list(value = 0))

p_cat_adj_heat <- ggplot(cat_adj, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))), size = 3, color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F0E5D2", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap de adyacencia",
    subtitle = "La forma más rápida de leer una matriz de enlaces",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))

cat_nodes_line <- cat_nodes |>
  mutate(x = seq_len(n()), y = 0)

cat_arc <- cat_links |>
  left_join(cat_nodes_line, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(cat_nodes_line, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_cat_arc <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = cat_arc,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.56
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.5, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = cat_nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(cat_nodes)], cat_nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Útil si los nodos tienen un orden lineal"
  )

cat_nodes_circle <- cat_nodes |>
  mutate(
    angle = seq(pi / 2, pi / 2 + 2 * pi - 2 * pi / n(), length.out = n()),
    x = cos(angle),
    y = sin(angle)
  )

cat_chord <- cat_links |>
  left_join(cat_nodes_circle, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(cat_nodes_circle, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_cat_chord <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = cat_chord,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.23,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.6, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = cat_nodes_circle, aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name), size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(cat_nodes)], cat_nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Resalta conectividad global entre categorías"
  )

cat_pos <- tibble(
  name = c("Ventas", "Marketing", "Producto", "Operaciones", "Soporte", "Finanzas"),
  x = c(0.0, 1.0, 1.8, 2.5, 0.6, 3.1),
  y = c(0.7, 1.7, 0.8, -0.1, -0.7, 0.2)
)

cat_network <- cat_links |>
  left_join(cat_pos, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(cat_pos, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_cat_network <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = cat_network,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value),
    color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.7)
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_pos, aes(x, y), size = 7, shape = 21, fill = PAL_MUST, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(data = cat_pos, aes(x, y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La vista más directa cuando los enlaces son el dato central"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-bar
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Categorías anidadas: barplot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(nested_counts, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = class)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  labs(
    title = "Barplot anidado",
    subtitle = "Primero compara el total y luego la composición interna",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Clase"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-pack
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Categorías anidadas: circular packing"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_nested_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(manufacturer, class), fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = distinct(pack_nested, manufacturer, x, y),
    aes(x, y, label = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_DARK,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.2
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing anidado",
    subtitle = "Expone inclusión jerárquica, no comparación exacta"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-sun
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Categorías anidadas: sunburst"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_rect(
    data = inner_nested,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = outer_nested,
    aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.3,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Jerarquía expresada en anillos concéntricos"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-nested-tree
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Categorías anidadas: treemap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(tree_nested) +
  geom_rect(
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = manufacturer),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = manufacturer),
    size = 3.2,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = PAL_DARK
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Compacta jerarquías con muy buen uso del área"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-grouped
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: grouped scatter"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(cross_counts, aes(value, class, color = drv)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Grouped scatter",
    subtitle = "Comparación ligera entre subgrupos",
    x = "Conteo",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Tracción"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-heat
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: heatmap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot(cross_counts, aes(drv, class, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), color = PAL_DARK, fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F2E7D3", high = PAL_MUST) +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap",
    subtitle = "Hace visible una tabla cruzada en segundos",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Conteo"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-lollipop
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: lollipop"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(cross_counts, aes(value, class, color = drv)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = value, y = class, yend = class),
    linewidth = 1.1,
    color = PAL_GRID,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3.8, position = position_dodge(width = 0.45)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Lollipop por subgrupo",
    subtitle = "Útil cuando quieres comparar pocos subgrupos por categoría",
    x = "Conteo",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Tracción"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-parallel
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: parallel plot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(profile_scaled, aes(drv, value_scaled, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Parallel plot",
    subtitle = "Compara el perfil relativo de cada categoría principal",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Escala relativa",
    color = NULL
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-spider
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: spider plot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(spider_cat, aes(drv, value_scaled, group = manufacturer, color = manufacturer, fill = manufacturer)) +
  geom_polygon(alpha = 0.12, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2.2) +
  coord_polar() +
  scale_color_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Spider plot",
    subtitle = "Muy llamativo, menos preciso que el paralelo",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    color = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-gbar
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: grouped barplot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(profile_categ, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = drv)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), width = 0.66) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Grouped barplot",
    subtitle = "La opción robusta para comparación entre subgrupos",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Tracción"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-sbar
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: stacked barplot"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 4.8

ggplot(profile_categ, aes(manufacturer, value, fill = drv)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.68) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = drv_cols2) +
  labs(
    title = "Stacked barplot",
    subtitle = "Sirve mejor para composición que para comparación fina",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Conteo",
    fill = "Tracción"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-subgroup-sankey
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Subgrupos: sankey"
#| fig-width: 7.8
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_prof,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(manufacturer, drv), fill = manufacturer),
    alpha = 0.78,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_prof, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_prof, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_prof, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = manufacturer), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_prof, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = drv), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = manu_cols2, guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey diagram",
    subtitle = "Muestra cómo se reparte cada categoría principal entre subgrupos"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-adyacencia-heat
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Adyacencia: heatmap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(cat_adj, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))), color = PAL_DARK, size = 3.1) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F2E7D3", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap de adyacencia",
    subtitle = "Resume conexiones entre categorías en forma matricial",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-adyacencia-arc
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Adyacencia: arc"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 3.9

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = cat_arc,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.56
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.5, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = cat_nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(cat_nodes)], cat_nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Útil si los nodos tienen un orden lineal"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-adyacencia-chord
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Adyacencia: chord"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = cat_chord,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.23,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.6, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = cat_nodes_circle, aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name), size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(cat_nodes)], cat_nodes$name)) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Resalta conectividad global entre categorías"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-categ-adyacencia-network
#| fig-cap: "Atlas categórico · Adyacencia: network"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = cat_network,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value),
    color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.7)
  ) +
  geom_point(data = cat_pos, aes(x, y), size = 7, shape = 21, fill = PAL_MUST, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(data = cat_pos, aes(x, y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La vista más directa cuando los enlaces son el dato central"
  )
```

## Cómo leer el atlas categórico

`Primera opción` en datos categóricos:

-   `barplot` y `lollipop` para frecuencia, ranking y comparación directa.
-   `heatmap` para tablas cruzadas o matrices de adyacencia.
-   `treemap` cuando necesitas composición compacta.
-   `venn` solo si el mensaje principal es el solapamiento entre pocos conjuntos.

`Especializados` en datos categóricos:

-   `waffle` cuando quieres enfatizar proporción con una base de 100 unidades.
-   `pie`, `doughnut`, `word cloud` y `circular packing` cuando aceptas perder precisión a cambio de forma expresiva.
-   `sunburst` y `dendrogram` cuando la estructura jerárquica es más importante que la comparación exacta.
-   `arc`, `chord`, `network` y `sankey` cuando la relación entre categorías pesa más que las categorías aisladas.

::: callout-tip
## Regla de decisión

Si el dato es puramente categórico, elige primero la estructura: frecuencia, solapamiento, jerarquía, cruce o red. Esa elección te deja muy pocas opciones realmente defendibles.
:::

# 14 · Atlas de gráficos relacionales

La familia relacional se centra en nodos, conexiones, jerarquías y flujos. Aquí lo importante no es solo el valor, sino quién está conectado con quién, qué tan fuerte es ese vínculo y si la estructura es plana, jerárquica o de red.

Este atlas es el más cercano al de categorías anidadas y al de adyacencia del apartado anterior. La diferencia editorial es simple: aquí conviene pensar primero en la forma de la estructura relacional y recién después en el gráfico concreto.

En términos de lectura, este es el atlas de referencia para `heatmap` relacional, `arc`, `chord`, `network` y `sankey`. Cuando esas familias reaparecen en otros apartados, deben leerse como adaptaciones de esta gramática general a datos categóricos o categórico-numéricos.

## Redes y matrices de conexión

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-a
#| include: false

rel_links <- tribble(
  ~from,        ~to,          ~value,
  "Cliente",    "Ventas",     9,
  "Cliente",    "Soporte",    6,
  "Ventas",     "Producto",   7,
  "Soporte",    "Producto",   4,
  "Producto",   "Operaciones",8,
  "Operaciones","Finanzas",   5,
  "Ventas",     "Finanzas",   3
)

rel_nodes <- tibble(name = unique(c(rel_links$from, rel_links$to)))

rel_adj <- bind_rows(
  rel_links,
  transmute(rel_links, from = to, to = from, value = value)
) |>
  complete(from = rel_nodes$name, to = rel_nodes$name, fill = list(value = 0))

p_rel_heat <- ggplot(rel_adj, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))), size = 3, color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F0E6D5", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap",
    subtitle = "Traduce una matriz relacional en patrón visible",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))

hive_nodes <- tribble(
  ~name,         ~axis,       ~r,
  "Cliente",     "Origen",    1.15,
  "Ventas",      "Proceso",   0.75,
  "Soporte",     "Proceso",   1.05,
  "Producto",    "Proceso",   0.45,
  "Operaciones", "Destino",   0.7,
  "Finanzas",    "Destino",   1.1
) |>
  mutate(
    angle = case_match(
      axis,
      "Origen" ~ pi / 2,
      "Proceso" ~ 7 * pi / 6,
      "Destino" ~ 11 * pi / 6
    ),
    x = r * cos(angle),
    y = r * sin(angle)
  )

hive_axes <- tibble(
  axis = c("Origen", "Proceso", "Destino"),
  angle = c(pi / 2, 7 * pi / 6, 11 * pi / 6)
) |>
  mutate(xend = 1.32 * cos(angle), yend = 1.32 * sin(angle))

hive_edges <- rel_links |>
  left_join(hive_nodes |> select(name, x0 = x, y0 = y), by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  left_join(hive_nodes |> select(name, x1 = x, y1 = y), by = c("to" = "name"))

p_rel_hive <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = hive_axes, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = xend, yend = yend), color = PAL_GRID, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_curve(
    data = hive_edges,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(data = hive_nodes, aes(x, y, fill = axis), size = 5, shape = 21, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data = hive_nodes, aes(1.12 * x, 1.12 * y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Origen" = PAL_CAN, "Proceso" = "#8393C6", "Destino" = PAL_MUST), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Hive",
    subtitle = "Ordena nodos por ejes estructurales y reduce cruces arbitrarios"
  )

rel_nodes_line <- rel_nodes |>
  mutate(x = seq_len(n()), y = 0)

rel_arc <- rel_links |>
  left_join(rel_nodes_line, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(rel_nodes_line, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_rel_arc <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = rel_arc,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.56
  ) +
  geom_point(data = rel_nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.4, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = rel_nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Funciona bien si existe un orden natural de los nodos"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-b
#| include: false

rel_nodes_circle <- rel_nodes |>
  mutate(
    angle = seq(pi / 2, pi / 2 + 2 * pi - 2 * pi / n(), length.out = n()),
    x = cos(angle),
    y = sin(angle)
  )

rel_chord <- rel_links |>
  left_join(rel_nodes_circle, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(rel_nodes_circle, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_rel_chord <- ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = rel_chord,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.22,
    alpha = 0.52
  ) +
  geom_point(data = rel_nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.6, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = rel_nodes_circle, aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name), size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Subraya la conectividad global entre nodos"
  )

rel_pos <- tibble(
  name = c("Cliente", "Ventas", "Soporte", "Producto", "Operaciones", "Finanzas"),
  x = c(0.0, 1.1, 0.7, 1.8, 2.6, 3.2),
  y = c(0.5, 1.4, -0.7, 0.4, -0.1, 0.8)
)

rel_network <- rel_links |>
  left_join(rel_pos, by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  rename(x0 = x, y0 = y) |>
  left_join(rel_pos, by = c("to" = "name")) |>
  rename(x1 = x, y1 = y)

p_rel_network <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = rel_network, aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value), color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.72)) +
  geom_point(data = rel_pos, aes(x, y), size = 7, shape = 21, fill = PAL_MUST, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(data = rel_pos, aes(x, y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La opción general para relaciones nodo-enlace"
  )

left_rel <- rel_links |>
  group_by(from) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_rel <- rel_links |>
  left_join(left_rel, by = c("from")) |>
  group_by(from) |>
  arrange(to, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymin0 = ymin + cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
    ymax0 = ymin0 + value
  ) |>
  ungroup()

right_rel <- rel_links |>
  group_by(to) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_rel2 <- flow_rel |>
  left_join(right_rel |> rename(ymin_r = ymin, ymax_r = ymax), by = c("to")) |>
  group_by(to) |>
  arrange(from, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymin1 = ymin_r + cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
    ymax1 = ymin1 + value
  ) |>
  ungroup()

sankey_rel <- pmap_dfr(
  list(flow_rel2$from, flow_rel2$to, flow_rel2$ymin0, flow_rel2$ymax0, flow_rel2$ymin1, flow_rel2$ymax1),
  function(from, to, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) {
    make_flow_ribbon(0.1, 0.9, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) |>
      mutate(from = from, to = to)
  }
)

p_rel_sankey <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_rel,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(from, to), fill = from),
    alpha = 0.78,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_rel, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_rel, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_rel, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = from), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_rel, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = to), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(left_rel)], left_rel$from), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey",
    subtitle = "Ideal para leer transferencias y reparto entre etapas"
  )

```

## Estructuras jerárquicas relacionales

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-a
#| include: false

hier_df <- tribble(
  ~grupo,        ~subgrupo,       ~value,
  "Clientes",    "Nuevos",        18,
  "Clientes",    "Recurrentes",   26,
  "Operación",   "Producción",    22,
  "Operación",   "Logística",     15,
  "Finanzas",    "Cobranza",      11,
  "Finanzas",    "Planeación",     9
)

group_totals <- hier_df |>
  group_by(grupo) |>
  summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop")

group_cols3 <- setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(group_totals)], group_totals$grupo)

pack_hier <- hier_df |>
  mutate(
    x = case_match(grupo, "Clientes" ~ 0.24, "Operación" ~ 0.73, "Finanzas" ~ 0.5),
    y = case_match(grupo, "Clientes" ~ 0.67, "Operación" ~ 0.64, "Finanzas" ~ 0.24),
    dx = c(-0.08, 0.08, -0.08, 0.08, -0.07, 0.07),
    dy = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),
    r = rescale(sqrt(value), to = c(0.09, 0.18))
  )

pack_hier_poly <- pmap_dfr(
  list(pack_hier$subgrupo, pack_hier$x + pack_hier$dx, pack_hier$y + pack_hier$dy, pack_hier$r, pack_hier$grupo),
  function(subgrupo, x0, y0, r, grupo) {
    circle_polygon(x0, y0, r) |>
      mutate(subgrupo = subgrupo, grupo = grupo)
  }
)

p_rel_pack <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_hier_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = subgrupo, fill = grupo),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  geom_text(data = distinct(pack_hier, grupo, x, y), aes(x, y, label = grupo), size = 3.2, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing",
    subtitle = "Jerarquía radial basada en contención visual"
  )

inner_rel <- group_totals |>
  mutate(
    ymax = cumsum(total),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0),
    xmin = 1,
    xmax = 2
  )

outer_rel <- hier_df |>
  left_join(inner_rel |> select(grupo, ymin_inner = ymin), by = "grupo") |>
  group_by(grupo) |>
  arrange(desc(value), .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymax = ymin_inner + cumsum(value),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = first(ymin_inner)),
    xmin = 2,
    xmax = 3
  ) |>
  ungroup()

p_rel_sun <- ggplot() +
  geom_rect(data = inner_rel, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_rect(data = outer_rel, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.82) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Adecuado para jerarquías con pocos niveles"
  )

tree_rel <- simple_treemap_layout(
  group_totals |>
    transmute(grupo, value = total),
  value_col = "value"
)

p_rel_tree <- ggplot(tree_rel) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = grupo), size = 3.3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Aprovecha mejor el espacio cuando el valor está en las hojas"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-b
#| include: false

leaf_profile <- hier_df |>
  mutate(subgrupo = factor(subgrupo, levels = subgrupo)) |>
  mutate(
    Clientes = if_else(grupo == "Clientes", value, 0),
    Operación = if_else(grupo == "Operación", value, 0),
    Finanzas = if_else(grupo == "Finanzas", value, 0)
  ) |>
  select(subgrupo, Clientes, Operación, Finanzas)

hc_rel <- hclust(
  dist(scale(select(leaf_profile, -subgrupo))),
  method = "ward.D2"
)

left_hier <- group_totals |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_hier <- hier_df |>
  left_join(left_hier, by = c("grupo")) |>
  group_by(grupo) |>
  arrange(subgrupo, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    ymin0 = ymin + cumsum(lag(value, default = 0)),
    ymax0 = ymin0 + value
  ) |>
  ungroup()

right_hier <- hier_df |>
  transmute(subgrupo, total = value) |>
  arrange(desc(total)) |>
  mutate(ymax = cumsum(total), ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0))

flow_hier2 <- flow_hier |>
  left_join(right_hier |> rename(ymin_r = ymin, ymax_r = ymax), by = c("subgrupo")) |>
  mutate(ymin1 = ymin_r, ymax1 = ymax_r)

sankey_hier <- pmap_dfr(
  list(flow_hier2$grupo, flow_hier2$subgrupo, flow_hier2$ymin0, flow_hier2$ymax0, flow_hier2$ymin1, flow_hier2$ymax1),
  function(grupo, subgrupo, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) {
    make_flow_ribbon(0.1, 0.9, ymin0, ymax0, ymin1, ymax1) |>
      mutate(grupo = grupo, subgrupo = subgrupo)
  }
)

p_rel_hier_sankey <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_hier,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(grupo, subgrupo), fill = grupo),
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_hier, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_hier, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_hier, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = grupo), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_hier, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = subgrupo), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey jerárquico",
    subtitle = "Convierte una jerarquía en una lectura de reparto"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-heat
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: heatmap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(rel_adj, aes(from, to, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = if_else(value == 0, "", as.character(value))), size = 3.1, color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F2E7D3", high = PAL_MUST) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap",
    subtitle = "Convierte una red en una matriz de relaciones",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Peso"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-hive
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: hive"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = hive_axes, aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = xend, yend = yend), color = PAL_GRID, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_curve(
    data = hive_edges,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.55
  ) +
  geom_point(data = hive_nodes, aes(x, y, fill = axis), size = 5, shape = 21, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data = hive_nodes, aes(1.12 * x, 1.12 * y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Origen" = PAL_CAN, "Proceso" = "#8393C6", "Destino" = PAL_MUST), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Hive",
    subtitle = "Ordena nodos por ejes estructurales y reduce cruces arbitrarios"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-arc
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: arc"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 3.9

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = rel_arc,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.28,
    alpha = 0.56
  ) +
  geom_point(data = rel_nodes_line, aes(x, y), size = 3.4, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = rel_nodes_line, aes(x, y - 0.1, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.16, 0.38), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Arc",
    subtitle = "Funciona bien si existe un orden natural de los nodos"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-chord
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: chord"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_curve(
    data = rel_chord,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value, color = from),
    curvature = 0.22,
    alpha = 0.52
  ) +
  geom_point(data = rel_nodes_circle, aes(x, y), size = 3.6, color = PAL_DARK) +
  geom_text(data = rel_nodes_circle, aes(1.18 * x, 1.18 * y, label = name), size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(rel_nodes)], rel_nodes$name), guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.2), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Chord",
    subtitle = "Subraya la conectividad global entre nodos"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-network
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: network"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = rel_network, aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = x1, yend = y1, linewidth = value), color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.72)) +
  geom_point(data = rel_pos, aes(x, y), size = 7, shape = 21, fill = PAL_MUST, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 1) +
  geom_text(data = rel_pos, aes(x, y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_linewidth(range = c(0.7, 2.4), guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Network",
    subtitle = "La opción general para relaciones nodo-enlace"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-redes-sankey
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Redes y matrices: sankey"
#| fig-width: 7.8
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_rel,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(from, to), fill = from),
    alpha = 0.78,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_rel, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_rel, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_rel, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = from), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_rel, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = to), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:nrow(left_rel)], left_rel$from), guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey",
    subtitle = "Ideal para leer transferencias y reparto entre etapas"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-pack
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Estructura anidada: circular packing"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = pack_hier_poly,
    aes(x, y, group = subgrupo, fill = grupo),
    color = PAL_BG,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.82
  ) +
  geom_text(data = distinct(pack_hier, grupo, x, y), aes(x, y, label = grupo), size = 3.2, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Circular packing",
    subtitle = "Jerarquía radial basada en contención visual"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-sun
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Estructura anidada: sunburst"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot() +
  geom_rect(data = inner_rel, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_rect(data = outer_rel, aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.82) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0, 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sunburst",
    subtitle = "Adecuado para jerarquías con pocos niveles"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-tree
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Estructura anidada: treemap"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

ggplot(tree_rel) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = grupo), color = PAL_BG, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(x = (xmin + xmax) / 2, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = grupo), size = 3.3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Treemap",
    subtitle = "Aprovecha mejor el espacio cuando el valor está en las hojas"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-dendro
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Estructura anidada: dendrograma"
#| fig-width: 7.2
#| fig-height: 5.2

plot(
  hc_rel,
  labels = leaf_profile$subgrupo,
  main = "Dendrograma",
  xlab = "",
  sub = ""
)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-nested-sankey
#| fig-cap: "Atlas relacional · Estructura anidada: sankey"
#| fig-width: 7.8
#| fig-height: 5.4

ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = sankey_hier,
    aes(x, y, group = interaction(grupo, subgrupo), fill = grupo),
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_rect(data = left_hier, aes(xmin = 0.02, xmax = 0.08, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_rect(data = right_hier, aes(xmin = 0.92, xmax = 0.98, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), fill = PAL_NO, color = PAL_BG) +
  geom_text(data = left_hier, aes(x = 0, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = grupo), hjust = 1, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data = right_hier, aes(x = 1, y = (ymin + ymax) / 2, label = subgrupo), hjust = 0, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols3, guide = "none") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-0.16, 1.16), clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Sankey jerárquico",
    subtitle = "Convierte una jerarquía en una lectura de reparto"
  )
```

## Conexiones densas

Cuando el problema principal es el exceso de enlaces, una alternativa es agruparlos visualmente en haces.

```{r}
#| label: fig-atlas-rel-edgebundling
#| fig-cap: "Relacional: edge bundling"
#| fig-width: 8.5
#| fig-height: 6

bundle_nodes <- tibble(
  name = c("A1", "A2", "B1", "B2", "C1", "C2", "D1", "D2"),
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C", "D", "D"),
  angle = c(120, 150, 30, 60, -30, -60, -120, -150) * pi / 180
) |>
  mutate(
    x = cos(angle),
    y = sin(angle)
  )

bundle_edges <- tribble(
  ~from, ~to,
  "A1", "B1",
  "A2", "B2",
  "A1", "C1",
  "A2", "C2",
  "B1", "D1",
  "B2", "D2",
  "C1", "D1",
  "C2", "D2"
) |>
  left_join(bundle_nodes |> select(name, group_from = group, x0 = x, y0 = y), by = c("from" = "name")) |>
  left_join(bundle_nodes |> select(name, group_to = group, x1 = x, y1 = y), by = c("to" = "name"))

group_anchor <- tibble(
  group = c("A", "B", "C", "D"),
  angle = c(135, 45, -45, -135) * pi / 180
) |>
  mutate(xc = 0.22 * cos(angle), yc = 0.22 * sin(angle))

bundle_df <- bundle_edges |>
  left_join(group_anchor |> rename(group_from = group, xcf = xc, ycf = yc), by = "group_from") |>
  left_join(group_anchor |> rename(group_to = group, xct = xc, yct = yc), by = "group_to")

bundle_paths <- pmap_dfr(
  list(bundle_df$from, bundle_df$to, bundle_df$x0, bundle_df$y0, bundle_df$x1, bundle_df$y1, bundle_df$xcf, bundle_df$ycf, bundle_df$xct, bundle_df$yct),
  function(from, to, x0, y0, x1, y1, xcf, ycf, xct, yct) {
    p1 <- quad_bezier(x0, y0, xcf, ycf, 0, 0, n = 50)
    p2 <- quad_bezier(0, 0, xct, yct, x1, y1, n = 50)
    bind_rows(p1, p2) |>
      mutate(edge = paste(from, to, sep = "_"))
  }
)

ggplot() +
  geom_path(
    data = bundle_paths,
    aes(x, y, group = edge),
    color = alpha(PAL_MID, 0.5),
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(data = bundle_nodes, aes(x, y, fill = group), size = 5, shape = 21, color = PAL_BG, stroke = 0.9) +
  geom_text(data = bundle_nodes, aes(1.12 * x, 1.12 * y, label = name), size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = PAL_DARK) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(cat_palette[1:4], c("A", "B", "C", "D")), guide = "none") +
  coord_equal(clip = "off") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Edge bundling",
    subtitle = "Agrupa trayectorias similares para reducir ruido visual en redes densas"
  )
```

## Cómo leer el atlas relacional

`Primera opción` en datos relacionales:

-   `heatmap` si la estructura puede escribirse como matriz y quieres lectura rápida.
-   `network` si necesitas una vista nodo-enlace general.
-   `sankey` si el mensaje principal es flujo entre etapas o bloques.
-   `treemap` o `dendrogram` si la estructura es claramente jerárquica.

`Especializados` en datos relacionales:

-   `hive`, `arc` y `chord` cuando el orden de nodos o la forma circular ayuda realmente a leer la red.
-   `circular packing` y `sunburst` cuando la jerarquía debe leerse más como inclusión que como comparación exacta.
-   `edge bundling` cuando la densidad de conexiones vuelve ilegible una red convencional.

::: callout-tip
## Regla de decisión

En estructuras relacionales, decide primero si tu dato es una matriz, una red, un flujo o una jerarquía. Luego elige la geometría que mejor reduzca cruces y carga cognitiva.
:::

# 15 · Plantilla reusable para tu propio flujo

La siguiente plantilla resume una forma de trabajo robusta para gráficos de análisis o comunicación.

``` r
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

datos_resumen <- datos |>
  mutate(...) |>
  filter(...) |>
  group_by(...) |>
  summarise(...)

datos_resumen |>
  ggplot(aes(x = ..., y = ..., color = ..., fill = ...)) +
  geom_*(
    alpha = ...,
    size = ...,
    linewidth = ...
  ) +
  geom_*() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(...)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(...)) +
  labs(
    title = "Conclusión principal",
    subtitle = "Contexto breve para orientar la lectura",
    x = "Eje X",
    y = "Eje Y",
    caption = "Fuente: ..."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

## Checklist antes de dar por terminado un gráfico

-   ¿El tipo de gráfico responde a la pregunta analítica?
-   ¿Las variables están bien mapeadas a posición, color y tamaño?
-   ¿Los ejes tienen unidades, breaks y rango adecuados?
-   ¿Las categorías están ordenadas si la comparación lo exige?
-   ¿El título explica qué hay que mirar?
-   ¿La leyenda realmente hace falta o conviene etiquetar directo?
-   ¿La paleta funciona con pocos colores y suficiente contraste?
-   ¿Las etiquetas, ejes y leyendas reducen trabajo al lector?
-   ¿El gráfico sigue funcionando en escala de grises o con impresión en PDF?

# 16 · Fuentes de estudio recomendadas

Esta guía se apoyó en los siguientes materiales, combinando principios de diseño, gramática de los gráficos y práctica de refinamiento:

-   [Fundamentals of Data Visualization](https://clauswilke.com/dataviz/)
-   [From Data to Viz](https://www.data-to-viz.com/)
-   [posit-conf-2023/dataviz-ggplot2](https://github.com/posit-conf-2023/dataviz-ggplot2)
-   [ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis](https://ggplot2-book.org/)
-   [A ggplot2 tutorial for beautiful plotting in R](https://www.cedricscherer.com/2019/08/05/a-ggplot2-tutorial-for-beautiful-plotting-in-r/)
-   [Top 50 ggplot2 Visualizations](https://r-statistics.co/Top50-Ggplot2-Visualizations-MasterList-R-Code.html)

## Cierre

Aprender `ggplot2` no consiste en memorizar muchas funciones. Consiste en practicar una secuencia estable: entender variables, elegir una forma visual adecuada, construir por capas y refinar hasta que el gráfico sea claro, honesto y fácil de leer.
